title: Fixing TypeScript Type Inference for Custom Objects

statement.markdown:
Given a custom object `Person` with properties `name` and `age`, fix the type inference issue in the `createPerson` function to return a `Person` object correctly.

The function takes two parameters: `name` and `age`, and returns an object with the same properties.

Example usage: `createPerson('John Doe', 30)` should return `{ name: 'John Doe', age: 30 }`.
statement.notes: Type inference should work correctly without explicit type annotations.

examples.explanation: Create a new `Person` object with the given name and age.

hints.text: Check the return type of the `createPerson` function.

editorial.title: Fixing TypeScript Type Inference
editorial.intuition: Type inference should work automatically without explicit type annotations.
editorial.bruteForce: Try adding explicit type annotations to the function parameters and return type.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use the `interface` keyword to define the `Person` type, and let TypeScript infer the type of the object returned by the function.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The `createPerson` function should return an object with the correct type without any errors.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run of the corrected `createPerson` function.
commonMistakes: Not letting TypeScript infer the type

functionSignature.javascript:
function createPerson(name: string, age: number): Person {
